title: The Blind Spot: How Oligarchs Dominate Our Democracies | Buffett Book Talk with Jeffrey Winters
location: 720 University Place, 720 University Place, Evanston, IL
description:
From ancient empires to modern nation-states, extreme concentrations of wealth have shaped political power—but never quite like this. Today, the influence of oligarchs is exercised not in opposition to democracy, but through it. Even as inequality reaches historic extremes, democratic systems continue to function, and public revolt remains muted. Why do societies repeatedly vote to sustain arrangements that entrench their own exclusion?
Join us for a Buffett Book Talk with Jeffrey A. Winters, Professor of Political Science, Director of the Roberta Buffett Institute’s Equality Development & Globalization Studies (EDGS) Program, and author of The Blind Spot: How Oligarchs Dominate Our Democracies. In conversation with Edward L. Gibson, Kenneth Burgess Professor of Political Science, Winters will trace the evolution of oligarchy in the democratic era, arguing that the outsized power of the ultra-wealthy is not a breakdown of democracy but a feature embedded in its design. They will examine Winters’ concept of participatory inequality—a paradoxical condition in which nearly everyone participates freely in democratic processes, yet only a tiny fraction exercises meaningful economic and political power—and offer ideas for how societies might rebalance power and pursue more equitable futures.
Lunch will begin at 12:15 p.m.
Co-sponsored by the Department of Political Science.
Please note that 720 University Place is not an ADA-accessible space. Increasing physical access to buildings and facilities is a goal of the University, but not all buildings and venues have been updated.

At The Infinite Wrench you’ll see 30 original plays in 60 minutes written and performed by The Neo-Futurist Ensemble. Each play offers something different, be it funny, profound, elegant, disgusting, topical, irreverent, terrifying, or a song; all are truthful and tackle the here-and-now.

title: Concert Band: Celebration
location: Pick-Staiger Concert Hall, 50 Arts Circle Drive, Evanston, IL
description:
Daniel J. Farris, conductor
Director of Athletic Bands Daniel J. Farris conducts his final concert after 26 years at Northwestern, where he led the “Wildcat” Marching Band and Concert Band. Prior to his time at Northwestern, Farris served as Assistant Director of Bands at Illinois State University and the University of Nevada, Las Vegas. He was also director of Orlando’s Walt Disney World Collegiate All-Star Band and taught in Minnesota and Wisconsin public schools. Bands under his direction have performed at the Macy’s Thanksgiving Day Parade, Bands of America Regional and National Championships, Ireland’s St. Patrick’s Day Parade in Dublin, and numerous bowl games. In this farewell concert, talented students from across the Northwestern campus present a range of exciting repertoire for band.

The Factory Theater's "The Sporting Life"
description:
The Factory Theater, 1623 W. Howard St., presents The Sporting Life, October 10 – November 22, written by Marjorie Muller and directed by Kayla Menz.

16-year-old Dot still hasn’t gotten her period. Lucky for her, there’s a Witch in the woods who can get it started. However, in exchange, Dot must offer her the life of a man who has wronged her. And who better to offer than a math teacher? When Dot gets her elusive first period and becomes witness to the Witch’s murder, they become bonded in literal blood, sending Dot down a path of self-understanding, bitterness, and perhaps a little bit of violence too \(as a treat\). The Sporting Life is a “coming of rage” story brutally snapped open to expose the, sometimes literal, entrails of growing up girl.

The performance schedule is October 10 – November 22  Fridays and Saturdays at 8 p.m., and Sundays at 3 p.m. There will be two additional Thursday performances November 13 and 20 at 8 p.m. An accessibility performance will occur on Sunday, Nov. 2. The Tickets are $10 – $30 and may be purchased through the box office by calling 312-275-5757 or by visiting TheFactoryTheater.com.

title: Hamdia Traoré’s “Des marabouts de Djenné” and Muslim Portraiture in Mali
location: Block Museum of Art, Mary and Leigh, 40 Arts Circle Drive, Evanston, IL
description:
The storied city of Djenné, a center of Islamic learning, study, and scholarship since the twelfth century, is the hometown of Bamako-based photographer Hamdia Traoré (b. 1992, Mali). The thirty portraits in Traore’s series Des marabouts de Djenné (Marabouts of Jenne) reflect his intimate connections to the city’s people and deep history. Learned and devout, marabouts teach in Djenné’s over 50 Qur’anic schools, offer spiritual guidance, and treat ailments through their knowledge of the Qur’an.
Made during a time of political and social upheaval in Mali, these portraits reflect enduring cultural resilience. Each image depicts a marabout seated with the tools of his practice—books, Qur’an boards, amulets, and prayer beads—framed by the architecture and atmosphere of Djenné. The consistent format underscores their collective identity, while individual poses and captions highlight personal roles and neighborhoods.
Traoré’s work will be shown alongside mid-20th-century black-and-white portraits of marabouts by Malian photographers Mamadou Cissé, Abdourahmane Sakaly, and Tijani Sitou. Drawn from the Archive of Malian Photography, these earlier images share visual parallels and deepen the historical context. Together, these images illuminate evolving perspectives on spiritual authority, identity, and visual representation in Mali.

Hamdia Traoré’s “Des marabouts de Djenné” and Muslim Portraiture in Mali” is curated by Kathleen Bickford Berzock, Associate Director of Curatorial Affairs, The Block Museum of Art, Northwestern University, in collaboration with Candace M. Keller, Associate Professor, Art History & Visual Culture, Michigan State University.  We are grateful to the Archive of Malian Photography (https://amp.matrix.msu.edu/), which provided digital scans of archival works. At The Block, the exhibition is supported by the Kadin/Spiegel Family Endowed Fund and a grant from the Illinois Arts Council.

title: Teresa Montoya’s Tó Łitso (Yellow Water): Ten Years after the Gold King Mine Spill
location: Block Museum of Art, Mary and Leigh, 40 Arts Circle Drive, Evanston, IL
description:
On August 5, 2015, the rupture of the abandoned Gold King Mine near Silverton, Colorado, released more than three million gallons of toxic wastewater into the Animas River, turning its waters a shocking shade of yellow. In the following year, artist and anthropologist Teresa Montoya (Diné, born 1984) embarked on a road trip from Silverton to Shiprock, New Mexico, retracing the path of the contaminated water and documenting its ongoing cultural, spiritual, and material effects on the Navajo Nation and other Indigenous communities downstream.
Marking the ten-year anniversary of the disaster, The Block Museum of Art partners with Montoya to revisit this journey. Tó Łitso (Yellow Water) explores the enduring consequences of the Gold King Mine spill through photography, sound recordings, water samples, and cartographic data.
Combining documentary photographs with scientific data and poetic reflection, Tó Łitso invites viewers to consider water not only as a life-sustaining resource but also as a conduit for histories, stories, and harm. In doing so, the exhibition challenges extractive frameworks of land use, centering Indigenous knowledge and resilience. Through Montoya’s interdisciplinary practice, Tó Łitso (Yellow Water) offers a powerful meditation on environmental and cultural justice in the Southwest and beyond.
"The 2015 spill had discharged more than three million gallons of acidic mine waste fluid into Cement Creek before joining the Animas River and eventually into the San Juan River, which flows directly across the Navajo Nation. Through my photographic journey, I make explicit the enduring presence of toxicity across multiple landscapes and territories. Sometimes it appears beautiful, other times haunting. The images highlight the relationships that various communities sustain through water/tó despite the occurrence of repeated and enduring contamination from upstream locales. The Gold King Mine Spill shows us this, even in that, which cannot be readily seen." – Teresa Montoya

title: SPINOFF
description:
Two years ago, Alé left the city to move back into their childhood home to caretake for their mother. Far from friends, their plans in the wind, Alé turned to the familiar world of TV sitcoms for comfort. That’s when Alé discovered Frasier.

Now, it’s time for Alé ’s SPINOFF. Using the script of Frasier as a roadmap, the cast of performers will explore this time in Alé’s life, deconstructing the sitcom form through the Neo-Futurist lens. Who are we to our parents, who are they to us, what’s changed in the years apart?

The parent and the child. The character and the person. Living under the same roof again, will they get picked up for syndication? Or canceled before they can tell the whole story?

location: Block Museum of Art, Mary and Leigh, 40 Arts Circle Drive, Evanston, IL
description:
The storied city of Djenné, a center of Islamic learning, study, and scholarship since the twelfth century, is the hometown of Bamako-based photographer Hamdia Traoré (b. 1992, Mali). The thirty portraits in Traore’s series Des marabouts de Djenné (Marabouts of Jenne) reflect his intimate connections to the city’s people and deep history. Learned and devout, marabouts teach in Djenné’s over 50 Qur’anic schools, offer spiritual guidance, and treat ailments through their knowledge of the Qur’an.
Made during a time of political and social upheaval in Mali, these portraits reflect enduring cultural resilience. Each image depicts a marabout seated with the tools of his practice—books, Qur’an boards, amulets, and prayer beads—framed by the architecture and atmosphere of Djenné. The consistent format underscores their collective identity, while individual poses and captions highlight personal roles and neighborhoods.
Traoré’s work will be shown alongside mid-20th-century black-and-white portraits of marabouts by Malian photographers Mamadou Cissé, Abdourahmane Sakaly, and Tijani Sitou. Drawn from the Archive of Malian Photography, these earlier images share visual parallels and deepen the historical context. Together, these images illuminate evolving perspectives on spiritual authority, identity, and visual representation in Mali.

Hamdia Traoré’s “Des marabouts de Djenné” and Muslim Portraiture in Mali” is curated by Kathleen Bickford Berzock, Associate Director of Curatorial Affairs, The Block Museum of Art, Northwestern University, in collaboration with Candace M. Keller, Associate Professor, Art History & Visual Culture, Michigan State University.  We are grateful to the Archive of Malian Photography (https://amp.matrix.msu.edu/), which provided digital scans of archival works. At The Block, the exhibition is supported by the Kadin/Spiegel Family Endowed Fund and a grant from the Illinois Arts Council.

title: Teresa Montoya’s Tó Łitso (Yellow Water): Ten Years after the Gold King Mine Spill
location: Block Museum of Art, Mary and Leigh, 40 Arts Circle Drive, Evanston, IL
description:
On August 5, 2015, the rupture of the abandoned Gold King Mine near Silverton, Colorado, released more than three million gallons of toxic wastewater into the Animas River, turning its waters a shocking shade of yellow. In the following year, artist and anthropologist Teresa Montoya (Diné, born 1984) embarked on a road trip from Silverton to Shiprock, New Mexico, retracing the path of the contaminated water and documenting its ongoing cultural, spiritual, and material effects on the Navajo Nation and other Indigenous communities downstream.
Marking the ten-year anniversary of the disaster, The Block Museum of Art partners with Montoya to revisit this journey. Tó Łitso (Yellow Water) explores the enduring consequences of the Gold King Mine spill through photography, sound recordings, water samples, and cartographic data.
Combining documentary photographs with scientific data and poetic reflection, Tó Łitso invites viewers to consider water not only as a life-sustaining resource but also as a conduit for histories, stories, and harm. In doing so, the exhibition challenges extractive frameworks of land use, centering Indigenous knowledge and resilience. Through Montoya’s interdisciplinary practice, Tó Łitso (Yellow Water) offers a powerful meditation on environmental and cultural justice in the Southwest and beyond.
"The 2015 spill had discharged more than three million gallons of acidic mine waste fluid into Cement Creek before joining the Animas River and eventually into the San Juan River, which flows directly across the Navajo Nation. Through my photographic journey, I make explicit the enduring presence of toxicity across multiple landscapes and territories. Sometimes it appears beautiful, other times haunting. The images highlight the relationships that various communities sustain through water/tó despite the occurrence of repeated and enduring contamination from upstream locales. The Gold King Mine Spill shows us this, even in that, which cannot be readily seen." – Teresa Montoya

title: Artist Talk - Quan Zhou, Artist in Residence
location: Kresge Hall, 1880 Campus Drive, Evanston, IL
description:
As the culminating event of her artist residency at Northwestern, Quan Zhou will present Linaje (Lineage), a transmedia installation that reconstructs the artist’s lost ancestry through AI-generated histories and artifacts—photographs, documents, drawings, and heirloom objects. In this public talk, she will discuss fundamental questions faced by first-generation descendants of migrants whose genealogies are erased, fragmented, or illegible.
This event is still in the planning stages; please check back closer to May 19 for full details!
Quan Zhou is the Spring 2026 Artist in Residence of the Department of Spanish and Portuguese and the Kaplan Humanities Institute.

title: Own the Interview: Preparation That Reduces Anxiety
description: Interviews feel uncertain, and uncertainty creates anxiety. This workshop shows how focused preparation reduces both. You’ll build a story vault of accomplishments to make your value clear; create confident first lines with powerful, intentional language; and sketch a simple 90-day plan that signals your readiness. You'll learn to apply powerful, intentional language so that you start your answers with confidence and how to implement a simple system with a workbook to stay consistent and in control.Presented on Zoom in partnership with the Lake Cook Career Collaborative. The presentation will be recorded and available for viewing afterward. Register and receive a Zoom link.

title: Talks@EHC The History of Chicago Homes, and What They Need Now
description:
Older Chicagoland homes were built to solve the problems of yesterday, but what do they need to thrive today? Join American Vintage Home for an engaging look at Chicago-area home history and the current homeowner challenges that older buildings often present. What was the reason for common original design choices such as radiators under windows, galvanized pipe, and basements? How can the average homeowner address the challenges that these same features create today, such as inefficient heat loss, inconsistent moisture, airflow and air quality issues, and aging systems that lead to water quality issues? We’ll focus on what the average homeowner can realistically do to preserve the character of older homes while improving comfort and efficiency. With Vicki Parker and Cornelius Muszczynski in partnership with American Vintage Home Community Education Series. FREE, RSVP recommended.

Part of Home: Evanston, an exploration of what it means to make a home, and feel at home, in Evanston.

title: Babel-On with R.F. Kuang
description: R.F. Kuang’s genre-bending fiction broaches ordinarily serious topics from a satirical and fantastical perspective. Academic yet approachable, Kuang’s work combines history, magic, and classical literary tradition to render powerful critiques of academia, the publishing industry, and even contemporary popular culture. Her most recent novel, Katabasis, follows two graduate students as they descend into hell after the death of their professor, and the screen rights options to the novel were sold to Amazon MGM Studios for an upcoming TV series before its publication. Kuang’s other bestselling titles include Yellowface, Babel, and The Poppy War trilogy, and she is the recipient of the Nebula Award for Best Novel, the Goodreads Choice Awards for Best Fiction, and the American Book Award. Kelly Jensen, an anti-censorship advocate, a senior editor at Book Riot, and writer who has compiled such anthologies as Here We Are: Feminism for the Real World and (Don’t) Call Me Crazy, will be joining Kuang in conversation.Save Your Zoom Seats

title: [producing body] Presents "Spaceman", May 19 - June 13 at Edge Off-Broadway
description:
\[producingbody\] is pleased to announce the Chicago premiere of Spaceman, by Leegrid Stevens and directed by Eric Slater, May 19 - June 13, at The Edge Off-Broadway, 1133 W. Catalpa Ave.

In Spaceman, the difficulties of interplanetary space travel are explored in exacting detail throughout this one-woman, sci-fi odyssey. Radiation exposure, long term weightlessness, low light, extensive communication delays and poor hygiene are just a few of the challenges astronaut Molly Jennis must face as the first human to visit Mars. But she soon finds nothing compares to the psychological effects of having so much time alone. Time spent mulling over the decisions she has made and what might be waiting for her when she finally reaches her destination.

The cast of Spaceman includes Ashley Neal \(she/her, Molly Jennis\); Allyce Torres \(she/her, ensemble puppeteer/Ares\) and Amy Carpenter \(she/her, Molly Jennis U/S\).

The production team includes Leegrid Stevens \(he/him, playwright\); Eric Slater \(he/him, director\); Amy Carpenter \(she/her, executive producer\); Taylor Dalton \(she/her, executive producer/set design/costume design\); Angela Joy Baldasare \(she/her, sound designer\) and Garrett Bell \(he/him, lighting designer\).

The Spaceman performance schedule is Thursdays and Fridays at 7:30 p.m., Saturdays at 4:30 and 7:30 p.m. and Sundays at 5 p.m. Ticket prices are $15-45. The running time is 100 minutes with no intermission. Tickets go on sale Friday, April 3 at 12 p.m. at producingbody.com.

title: Ride of Silence (David Barish)
description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Casual

Distance: 10 Miles

Leader: David Barish

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/RWGPS-route.png: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/13719785

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/ride%20of%20silence.jpgEvanston will again participate in the International Ride of Silence [](http://www.rideofsilence.org)http://www.rideofsilence.org. On this date riders all over the world will ride in silence to remember riders who have been injured or killed on the roads and to support cyclists and cycling. **Starts at: Wheel & Sprocket 1027 Davis Evanston.** Please be at Wheel and Sprocket before 7pm so we can all talk before we silently leave on the 11 mile ride. Wheel and Sprocket will host us for some refreshments after the ride. If you are riding home you will need a light as it will be dark by the time we are done. Need a light? You can get one at the shop. Rides all over the world leave at 7pm to honor and remember people injured or killed on the road. There is a listing for the ride at Ride_of_Silence-_Evanston: http://www.thechainlink.org/events/ride-of-silence-evanston-2. Come ride with us. This will be at a very casual pace.If you want to ride but are not near Evanston at 7pm you can try one of the other Rides of Silence in the Chicago area. They are also listed on the calendar on The Chainlink.

title: [producing body] Presents "Spaceman", May 19 - June 13 at Edge Off-Broadway
description:
\[producingbody\] is pleased to announce the Chicago premiere of Spaceman, by Leegrid Stevens and directed by Eric Slater, May 19 - June 13, at The Edge Off-Broadway, 1133 W. Catalpa Ave.

In Spaceman, the difficulties of interplanetary space travel are explored in exacting detail throughout this one-woman, sci-fi odyssey. Radiation exposure, long term weightlessness, low light, extensive communication delays and poor hygiene are just a few of the challenges astronaut Molly Jennis must face as the first human to visit Mars. But she soon finds nothing compares to the psychological effects of having so much time alone. Time spent mulling over the decisions she has made and what might be waiting for her when she finally reaches her destination.

The cast of Spaceman includes Ashley Neal \(she/her, Molly Jennis\); Allyce Torres \(she/her, ensemble puppeteer/Ares\) and Amy Carpenter \(she/her, Molly Jennis U/S\).

The production team includes Leegrid Stevens \(he/him, playwright\); Eric Slater \(he/him, director\); Amy Carpenter \(she/her, executive producer\); Taylor Dalton \(she/her, executive producer/set design/costume design\); Angela Joy Baldasare \(she/her, sound designer\) and Garrett Bell \(he/him, lighting designer\).

The Spaceman performance schedule is Thursdays and Fridays at 7:30 p.m., Saturdays at 4:30 and 7:30 p.m. and Sundays at 5 p.m. Ticket prices are $15-45. The running time is 100 minutes with no intermission. Tickets go on sale Friday, April 3 at 12 p.m. at producingbody.com.

title: Between the Lines: The Mighty Red by Louise Erdrich
location: 6140 Lincoln Avenue, Morton Grove, IL, 60053, US
description: Between the Lines is now held on two different days, either in person or on Zoom. Titles are always available on Hoopla, as well as other library ebook platforms. New members are warmly welcome! Book Synopsis: In this stunning novel, Pulitzer Prize and National Book Award–winning author Louise Erdrich tells a story of love, natural forces, spiritual yearnings, and the tragic impact of uncontrollable circumstances on ordinary people’s lives.In the Red River Valley of North Dakota, several lives revolve around a wedding fraught with desire, jealousy, and uncertainty. Gary Geist, a terrified young man set to inherit two farms, is desperate to marry Kismet Poe, an impulsive, lapsed goth who can’t read her own future but will settle for fulfilling his. Her best friend, Hugo, a gentle, red-haired, homeschooled giant, also loves Kismet and is determined to steal her away and build a life together. Kismet’s mother, Crystal, drives a truck for Gary’s family, and on her nightly runs, tunes in to the darkness of late-night radio, experiences visions of guardian angels, and worries about what’s to come, for her daughter and herself.The Mighty Red is Louise Erdrich at her consummate best. A novel of tender humor, disquietude, yearning, community, and family, it is about ordinary people who dream, grow up, fall in love, struggle, endure tragedy, carry bitter secrets; men and women both complicated and contradictory, flawed and decent, lonely and hopeful. Human time, deep time, Red River time, and geological time are explored alongside the impact of crises in our own time—climate change, the depletion of natural resources, the economic meltdown of 2008. It is a story about our tattered bond with the earth, and about love in all of its absurdity and splendor.

title: Thirsty Thursday
description: Thirsty Thursday is an event hosted by Sketchbook Brewing Company \(4901 Main Street, Skokie, IL\) in conjunction with the Evanston Homebrew Club. This gathering is meant to bring together homebrewers, potential homebrewers and craft beer ethusiasts to sample and evaluate homebrewed beers. Two of the founding principles of the Evanston HomebrewClub is to promote the craft of homebrewing and share our appreciation of craft beers. This event gives us an opportunity to showcase your latest batch, get some constructive feedback, and try out other brews as well. If you&\#39;re a homebrewer, feel free to bring a couple of samples to share with your friends. If you&\#39;re not homebrewing \(yet\), feel free to stop on by, enjoy a pint or two of Sketchbook&\#39;s latest creations, and make a friend or three.

title: Friday Film
description: HamnetThis lush, emotional historical drama—winner of the Golden Globe for Best Drama—is based on the critically acclaimed novel by Maggie O'Farrell, who cowrote the screenplay with Oscar-winning director Chloé Zhao. Set in 16th-century Tudor England, the film follows Agnes, a gifted healer, and her husband Will, whose marriage is pushed to the breaking point by the sudden death of their 11-year-old son, Hamnet, from the plague. Agnes grapples with a raw grief that threatens to consume her, while Will ultimately pours his profound sorrow into the creation of his immortal masterpiece, Hamlet.Starring Jessie Buckley, Paul Mescal, Emily Watson, and Joe Alwyn. Directed by Chloé Zhao. Hamnet received eight Academy Award nominations, including Best Picture, Director, Actress (Buckley), and Adapted Screenplay. 2025 : 126 minutes : Rated PG-13 for thematic content, some strong sexuality, and partial nudity : Historical dramaFree tickets will be given to those who attend.Watch a trailer for Hamnet.Find Hamnet in the library catalog.

title: Visit to Ft Sheridan (Ride Leader Needed)
description:
Status: RIDE LEADER NEEDED

Pace: Moderate

Distance: 30 Miles

Leader: Ride Leader Needed

# !https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/Ft%20Sheridan%20Cavalry%20Training.jpgWe will visit a few interesting locations inside Fort Sheridan

1\. There was an airfield at the fort used during WWII. We will visit its location on the ride.

2\. Anti-aircraft training included firing at drones over the lake. The anti-aircraft gun on display was actually designed and manufactured in Sweden.

3\. Training for the mounted Cavalry was a major activity at the fort until WWII. We will circle the "stable park" shown on the google map. Attached is a horsemanship photo from one of the displays. I found it both frightening and entertaining.

4\. There is a private home on the lake. It has a private road with electric gates.

Lunch will be at Once Upon a Bagel in downtown Highland Park. We will often ride paths rather than roads within the fort to get a different view. The pace will be on the low side of moderate. Part of the ride will be along the lakefront in the Southeast corner of the fort. We will have to walk our bikes over a short grass path.

Today, he is joined by a trio of talented instrumentalists.Free tickets will be given to those present starting at 2:30 pm.

title: Between the Lines: The Mighty Red by Louise Erdrich (Online)
description: Between the Lines meets for online discussions via Zoom. Registered patrons will receive an email the day before the program with the meeting ID and password for joining the event. New members are warmly welcome!Titles are always available on Hoopla, as well as other library ebook platforms. Contact us for assistance, or find any instructions you may need on our Download & Stream page.Book Synopsis: In this stunning novel, Pulitzer Prize and National Book Award–winning author Louise Erdrich tells a story of love, natural forces, spiritual yearnings, and the tragic impact of uncontrollable circumstances on ordinary people’s lives.In the Red River Valley of North Dakota, several lives revolve around a wedding fraught with desire, jealousy, and uncertainty. Gary Geist, a terrified young man set to inherit two farms, is desperate to marry Kismet Poe, an impulsive, lapsed goth who can’t read her own future but will settle for fulfilling his. Her best friend, Hugo, a gentle, red-haired, homeschooled giant, also loves Kismet and is determined to steal her away and build a life together. Kismet’s mother, Crystal, drives a truck for Gary’s family, and on her nightly runs, tunes in to the darkness of late-night radio, experiences visions of guardian angels, and worries about what’s to come, for her daughter and herself.The Mighty Red is Louise Erdrich at her consummate best. A novel of tender humor, disquietude, yearning, community, and family, it is about ordinary people who dream, grow up, fall in love, struggle, endure tragedy, carry bitter secrets; men and women both complicated and contradictory, flawed and decent, lonely and hopeful. Human time, deep time, Red River time, and geological time are explored alongside the impact of crises in our own time—climate change, the depletion of natural resources, the economic meltdown of 2008. It is a story about our tattered bond with the earth, and about love in all of its absurdity and splendor.

title: Fantasy Book Club

While fleeing, she meets the Crown Prince, who invites her to train alongside him in archery and magic.This fantasy book is inspired by the legend of the Chinese moon goddess, Chang'e, and is the first in the Celestial Kingdom duology.Find Daughter of the Moon Goddess in the library catalog.

title: Morning Movie
description: Elio Eleven-year-old Elio believes that extraterrestrials are out there and feels he’d fit in better with them than with people. One day, his dream comes true. He's taken to the Communiverse and mistaken for an ambassador from Earth. This fun action-packed cosmic misadventure explores themes of loneliness, belonging, grief, loss, family, acceptance, self-discovery, and identity. Elio and his space-alien best friend Glordon are endearing and relatable, and the animation is bright and imaginative. Featuring the voice talents of Yonas Kibreab, Remy Edgerly, Zoe Saldana, Brad Garrett, Jameela Jamil, Shirley Henderson, and Matthias Schweighofer. Directed by Madeline Sharafian, Domee Shi, and Adrian Molina. This film received an Oscar nomination for Best Animated Feature Film. 2025 : 98 minutes : Rated PG for some action/peril and thematic elements : AnimationFree tickets will be given to all who attend. Watch the trailer for Elio.Find Elio in the library catalog.

title: Pages Through History
description: This month we'll be discussing The Second Founding: How the Civil War and Reconstruction Remade the Constitution by Eric Foner. It would be hard for a person contemplating the United States of 1865 not to conclude that the system the Founders had bequeathed the nation just under a century earlier had been catastrophically flawed. Pulitzer Prize-winning historian Eric Foner lays out the ways the 13th, 14th, and 15th Amendments–all passed in just five years–represented an attempt to fix those flaws. They abolished slavery, codified the principles of birthright citizenship and legal equality, and guaranteed the right of Black men to vote. We live in the America the “Reconstruction Amendments” made, but we’ve been fighting about those amendments ever since.   Find The Second Founding in the library catalog.

description: Queen of GloryWriter-director-actor Nana Mensah does triple duty in this film. The story centers on Sarah Obeng (Mensah), a Ghanaian American PhD student in molecular neuro-oncology at Columbia University. She is about to relocate to Ohio with her married boyfriend when news comes that her mother has died unexpectedly. Sarah returns to the Bronx to settle her affairs, which include inheriting a Christian bookstore called King of Glory, and she finds herself navigating the expectations of Ghanaian relatives and the community while figuring out what she wants and needs. Starring Nana Mensah, Meeko Gattuso, Oberon K.A. Adjepong, Adam Leon, Russell G. Jones, and Anya Migdal. Directed by Nana Mensah.After watching the film, share your questions and comments in a discussion with Chris and Sharon.2021 : 78 minutes : not rated: Comedy-dramaFree tickets will be given to those who attend.Watch the trailer for Queen of Glory.Find Queen of Glory in the library catalog.

title: Mystery Book Club
description: This month we'll be discussing The Case of the Missing Maid by Rob Osler. As the Prescott Detective Agency’s only female operative, Harriet Morrow must work with what she’s given, an elderly, eccentric client certain that her housekeeper has met with foul play. But discovering the places in Chicago where women who like women and men who like men gather may force Ms. Morrow to face the parts of herself she keeps hidden. Set in 1898, this is a mystery rich in both local and queer history.  Find The Case of the Missing Maid in the library catalog.

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/RWGPS-route.png: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/9417618

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/Northwestern.jpgThis week's Thursday Casual Ride: Our casual loop ride starts with a beautiful view of Lake Michigan and the Chicago skyline from Northwestern University. We then head west and travel south on the McCormick bike path through the Skokie Sculpture Park. Next we head west on Pratt and wend our way to the "mosaic tunnel." We will then stop for a picnic in the forest preserve. Finally we return via the North Branch bike trail through the forest preserves then east via Glenview Road and the Wilmette Community Playfields. We will ride at a casual pace of 10-12 mph.

title: Friday Film
description: A Nice Indian BoyNaveen, a successful doctor, has always dreamed of having a Bollywood-style wedding like his sister’s. His guarded nature, however, has kept him from finding Mr. Right. Enter Jay: a white freelance photographer who was adopted by an Indian couple, speaks Hindi, and prays to Ganesha. They share a lot in common and quickly fall in love. But as Naveen introduces Jay to his traditional parents, they must face the ‘bigness of love’ and complexities of family and marriage.Starring Harish Patel, Jonathan Groff, Karan Soni, Peter S. Kim, Sunita Mani, and Zarna Garg. title: Time Capsule 2050 Sealing Ceremony
description: Skokie civic leaders will review materials collected from residents and local organizations from the year 2025 and seal them away in our community time capsule to be opened in 2050. Reception will follow. This is a joint project of the Skokie Historical Society and the Skokie Park District, in collaboration with Skokie Public Library. Free tickets will be given to those present starting at 1:30 pm.

title: Nature Play
description: Young children are invited to explore nature in the North Courtyard. Be prepared to dig in and get your hands messy.Drop-in event for children younger than six with an adult.

title: Monarch Community Science Project
description: Monarch butterflies play an important role as pollinators, and their populations are in trouble. You can learn about them and teach others, too.Tend our native garden patch, observe monarch eggs and caterpillars, and talk with other library patrons about your observations and how we can all support monarchs. Data will be submitted to the Monarch Larva Monitoring Project. All participants receive service hours as part of Teen Summer Service. Fill out this contact form to be added to our list of interested teen volunteers and be one of the first to know about volunteer opportunities.Email the teen team to learn more or to express an interest in volunteering. For grade 6-12.

title: Sensory-Friendly Family Movie
description: Strange WorldThis screening is designed for everyone, including families with neurodivergent children and others who may benefit from a less sensory-intense movie experience. We will keep the lights on and turn the sound down so you can shout, sing, or move about however you need to enjoy the film.In Strange World, a family of explorers deal with family conflicts while also adventuring into uncharted lands.2022 : 102 minutes : Rated PG for action/peril and some thematic elements : Animation, Adventure, Sci-FiFree tickets will be given to all who attend. Children younger than eight must be accompanied by an adult.Watch the trailer for Strange World.Find Strange World in the library catalog.

title: Rainbow Family Club

title: Music Monday
description: Explore how the LGBTQIA+ community helped shape modern music. We'll journey from disco to the birth of house music in Chicago and explore how underground queer culture inspired the beats and anthems that eventually conquered the global charts.Each Music Monday event includes a presentation related to music history, followed by discussion.

title: Middlefork Savanna (Neal Ney)
description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Moderate

Distance: 45 Miles

Leader: Neal Ney

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/RWGPS-route.png: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/27865884

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/MiddleforkSavanna.jpgThis week's Tuesday Moderate Ride: A savanna is open woodland with grasses and forbs growing under the trees. We will ride at a moderate pace to this rare remnant from Illinois' pre-settlement past. We'll ride a bit more than four miles on the limestone trail in the preserve at a slow pace with some stops for nature gawking. The trail is suitable for road bikes (as long as you are not in a hurry). After lunch at the Panera on 176, we will resume our moderate pace on the roads and paved paths back to Evanston.

title: Low Vision Group

description: Share your family's immigration story to help celebrate Immigrant Heritage Month. By seeing the history of our community through the forces that brought us into proximity--like opportunity, family ties, or refuge--we can appreciate where we come from, how migration changes us and our communities, and where we might be going. More opportunities to share: Wednesday, June 10, 3-5 pmMonday, June 15, 11 am-1 pm and 4-6 pmFriday, June 26, 11 am-1 pm and 2-4 pm

description:
Status: Confirmed

## Monthly Moderate Breakfast Club Ride:

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/5800%20North%20Caf%C3%A9.jpg: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/27045735If you like to eat, ride a bike, meet new people, explore different parts of the city but dedicate only half a day doing it at a time, this service of group bicycle rides is for you! The “Breakfast Club” will ride at a moderate pace to a different neighborhood stop for breakfast at a pre-selected restaurant, and be back around noon

Join us as we ride to breakfast at **5800 North Café**, 5800 N Milwaukee Ave, Chicago in the Jefferson Park neighborhood. Known for its homemade dishes, the cafe offers a delicious selection of pancakes, omelets, fresh hash browns, corned beef hash, and other brunch favorites.

title: Garrison Site in Lake Forest (Neal Ney)
description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Moderate

Distance: 40 miles

Leader: Neal Ney

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/Garrison%20Site%20Plaque.jpg
!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/RWGPS-route.png: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/36054459 A ride to an important but little known archeological site. It is marked today by only a simple plaque on a bolder, but it is neat to know that there were human settlements 10,000 years ago in the midst of the communities we regularly ride through.

The Garrison Site is an archaeological site in Lake Forest that was excavated in 1991-1992. It is located north of Westleigh Road near Wallace Road and the Carroll Meadow Nature Preserve and was excavated when a new road was put in by developer Barry Carroll.

The Garrison site yielded some of the oldest and best documented evidence of human occupation in Northeast Illinois. Archaeological evidence from the site shows that Paleo Indians were present in Lake Forest as long as 10,000 years ago, though most items found date to the Woodland Period. The site was named in honor of the late Morrow Garrison who had worked closely with Barry Carroll on various developments.

title: Songs of the Illinois Freedom Road
description: Award-winning folk musician and folklorist Chris Vallillo brings the music and the stories of the Underground Railroad in Illinois to life in this engaging and powerful show featuring first-person accounts of Freedom Seekers as well as performances and insights into the music that drove this historic movement. “While traditional accounts of the underground railroad generally dismiss Illinois, our state had more miles bordering slave states than any other” said Chris, “and those borders were rivers which made for excellent escape routes.”Chris Vallillo is a singer/songwriter and roots musician who makes the people and places of “unmetropolitan” America come to life in song. Having spent the last 30 years in the rural Midwest, he has a natural affinity for American roots music. He weaves original, contemporary, and traditional songs and narratives into a compelling and entertaining portrait of the history and lifestyles of the Midwest.Free tickets will be distributed to those present at 2:30 pm.

description: We Bought a ZooBenjamin Mee, recently widowed with two children—14-year-old Dylan and 7-year-old Rosie—thinks that starting over will do them all good, especially after Dylan gets expelled from school. While looking to relocate, they find a dilapidated old house that happens to come with a zoo that needs saving. There, they meet zookeeper Kelly Foster and her small, dedicated staff, who want to see it reopened. Despite the huge financial commitment and having no experience, Benjamin takes on the task with the help of his family.Loosely inspired by a true story, this is a healing adventure about second chances. title: Tour of Columbus Park (Rebecca Tung)
description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Casual

Distance: 20-25 miles

Leader: Rebecca Tung

## !https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/Columbus%20Park%20Chicago.jpgThis week's Thursday Casual Ride: Tour of Columbus Park

Unlike Chicago's west parklands, Columbus Park was created entirely by landscape architect Jens Jensen, who designed it between 1915 and 1920. Representing the culmination of his ideas, this 135-acre Prairie Style landscape seven miles west of downtown is considered his public masterpiece.

Columbus Park's design includes a meandering lagoon intended to emulate a "prairie river," complete with cascades and gentle waterfalls constructed of stratified stone. Jensen integrated a pre-existing nine-hole golf course into his plan and used native plants throughout, including hawthorn, oak, and linden trees. The meadow is flanked by a pair of clearings, framed by native vegetation, intended to serve as backstage dressing rooms. Columbus Park is the only park in Chicago designed by Jensen with his signature “council ring,” a circular stone bench for people to come together as equals to share ideas and experiences. Located on the lagoon's northwestern shore, a Mediterranean-revival style refectory, designed by Chatten and Hammond, was added in 1922.

title: Get to Know the Grand Old Flag
description: With the upcoming 4th of July celebrating the 250th anniversary of the United States, learn about the history of the U.S. flag with expert Marty Callahan. He'll share other historic flags that will inspire you as we make our own creations and parade them around the library. Parade will begin at 12:15 pm. Keyword: USA 250

title: Rohingya Music in Exile--Courage, Faith, and Love
description: The Crossing Borders Music String Quartet shares brand-new arrangements of Rohingya music and its stories of courage, faith, and love in exile. Developed after a month of field work in the Rohingya refugee camps of Cox's Bazar, Bangladesh-- the world's largest--this performance shares the melodies, hope, and humanity of Rohingya musicians who are the keepers of their people’s stories and cultural legacy. Transcend physical distance with music that helps everyone "see the Rohingya with our hearts" and celebrates our shared humanity. Free tickets will be distributed to those present at 2:30 pm.

title: The Constitution Today
description: Political analyst Paul Lisnek moderates a nonpartisan panel discussion with Chicagoland university professors. They'll discuss current issues around the First Amendment, power structure of the federal branches, and recent cases heard by the Supreme Court.About the Moderator: Paul Lisnek served as the political and legal analyst for WGN-TV for 18 years. Paul has been a lawyer for 40 years, is a former assistant dean and professor at Loyola Law School, and taught at DePaul University, University of Illinois in Urbana, and Pepperdine University. He teaches constitutional law and ethics for the lawyers bar exam. He was a disciplinary commissioner for lawyers for 25 years, and he served as a legal and jury expert for NBC News and CNN. PanelistsSteven D. Schwinn teaches constitutional law, First Amendment, comparative constitutional law and human rights, and lawyering skills for civil rights at UIC Law.Robert E. Shapiro is a trial lawyer and litigator at Barack Ferrazzano Kirschbaum & Nagelberg LLP with more than 40 years of experience. He handles commercial disputes in trial and appellate courts, ADR forums, and administrative and governmental proceedings nationwide and abroad. He is an adjunct professor of philosophy at Saint Xavier UniversityThis event is part of the Civic Empowerment Series 2026, in partnership with Schaumburg Township District Library, Vernon Area Public Library, Blue Island Public Library, Highland Park Public Library, Aurora Public Library District, La Grange Public Library, Fox River Valley Public Library, Algonquin Area Public Library District, and Oak Park Public Library. Register and receive a Zoom link for this event.For questions about this event, call Schaumburg Township District Library at 847-923-3347.

description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Moderate

Distance: 25 Miles

Leader: Will Heelan

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/Fireworks.jpg!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/RWGPS-route.png: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/43548306EBC Fireworks Enthusiasts: Wilmette will hold its annual Independence Day Fireworks Display on July 3rd at 9:30pm at the lakefront in Gillson Park and we are planning to be there! This moderate ride will **start at Panera Wilmette at 6 pm** to take a 20 mile scenic loop north, returning to Panera in Wilmette around 8 pm. After picking up our carry-out dinners we will proceed to the eastern lawn of the Baha'i Temple (just off the west public sidewalk on Sheridan Road south of the Wilmette Harbor bridge and about 90 feet north of Linden Avenue). There we will stake out our viewing location and enjoy our picnic while we wait for the fireworks to start. Feel free to join us for any or all of these activities. This ride is a great way to celebrate the Independence Day holiday! **Lights and helmets mandatory**, something to sit on highly recommended, (also consider bug repellant if you are susceptible).

title: Middlefork Savanna (Neal Ney)
description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Moderate

Distance: 45 Miles

Leader: Neal Ney

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/RWGPS-route.png: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/27865884

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/MiddleforkSavanna.jpgA savanna is open woodland with grasses and forbs growing under the trees. We will ride at a moderate pace to this rare remnant from Illinois' pre-settlement past. We'll ride a bit more than four miles on the limestone trail in the preserve at a slow pace with some stops for nature gawking. The trail is suitable for road bikes (as long as you are not in a hurry). After lunch at the Panera on 176, we will resume our moderate pace on the roads and paved paths back to Evanston.

title: Annual EBC Picnic (Mary Fry)
description:
Status: PENDING!

Leader: Mary Fry

## Our annual EBC picnic at Evanston's Lighthouse Park(Central/Sheridan):

The day begins with a morning ride and culminates in a potluck lunch. Check the Rides Calendar to find the perfect ride for you. All rides begin and end at the Lighthouse Beach Park. Please bring a dish to share that does not spoil easily, (no mayonnaise salads, raw meat/there is no grill). Beginning @ 8:00 AM, coolers and a watchful eye will be available for food. The club will provide soda, plates/eating utensils, and veggie and meat roll-ups.

Whether or not you ride, please come to the picnic. We always have a good time! (No alcoholic beverages. That would be a violation of park rules). Questions, suggestions, or volunteer to help, tele/text Mary Fry @ 773-383-2466.

title: Mucho Gusto Barra + Concina: Monthly Moderate Breakfast Club Ride (Sharon Kaminecki)
description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Moderate

Distance: Approx 20 miles

Leader: Sharon Kaminecki

## Monthly Moderate Breakfast Club Ride:

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/Mucho%20Gusto%20Barra%20%2B%20Concina.jpg: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/27045735If you like to eat, ride a bike, meet new people, explore different parts of the city but dedicate only half a day doing it at a time, this service of group bicycle rides is for you! The “Breakfast Club” will ride at a moderate pace to a different neighborhood stop for breakfast at a pre-selected restaurant, and be back around noon.

Join us as we amigos roll to breakfast at [](https://joedonut.com/locations/niles/)**Mucho Gusto Barra + Concina**, 2700 N Milwaukee Ave, Chicago, in the Logan Square neighborhood. Inspired by the colors and charm of San Miquel de Allende, the brunch menu includes breakfast burritos, chilaquiles, French toast, and huevos al bajio.

description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Moderate

Distance: Approx 20 miles

Leader: Sharon Kaminecki

## Monthly Moderate Breakfast Club Ride:

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/Sorelle%20Italian%20Market%20and%20Caf%C3%A9%20-%20Glen.jpg: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/27045735If you like to eat, ride a bike, meet new people, explore different parts of the city but dedicate only half a day doing it at a time, this service of group bicycle rides is for you! The “Breakfast Club” will ride at a moderate pace to a different neighborhood stop for breakfast at a pre-selected restaurant, and be back around noon.

Join us as we ride to breakfast at **Sorelle Italian Market and Café**, 1941 Tower Dr, Glenview. Menu features breakfast croissants, egg bites, and breakfast brioche along with Italian pastries

title: Drum, shake, make music.
description:
The Chicago Drum Circle Meetup Group
• What we'll do
Open, lightly facilitated, family friendly, community drum circle.

• What to bring
Bring yourself, a friend, and a hand drum, percussion instrument or a found sound.

• Important to know
This is a Ridgeville Park District partnership program. Alcohol, tobacco, vaping and drugs are strictly prohibited.

title: Gus's Drive-In, Wisconsin (Neal Ney)
description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Moderate

Distance: 49 Miles

Leader: Neal Ney

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/RWGPS-route.png: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/30880656

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/guss-drive-in-east-troy.jpgI love to ride in the SE Wisconsin countryside near Burlington, and Gus’s Drive-in is a favorite lunch spot. We have had fast and fast plus paced rides in this area for years. A few years back Ken Schulein put together a shorter route suitable for moderate riders that has become one of my favorite routes in this area. The route travels on rural roads with rolling hills to Gus's Drive-in, East Troy, WI for lunch at mile 25. Rest Stops with snacks are at mile 12 and Adrian's Frozen Custard in Burlington at mile 38.

Wisconsin Start: Brighton_Dale_Golf_Course: https://www.google.com/maps/place/Brighton+Dale+Links/@42.6305121%20-88.1179985%2012z/data=!4m2!3m1!1s0x8805790ac45286f9:0x24038a6eaef072f2. Take I-94 North. 7.5 miles past the Wisconsin border take Exit #340 WI-142 West toward Burlington. Turn left (west) at the end of the exit ramp and go west 8.5 miles on WI-142. Turn right (north) at WI-75 (248th St) and go 0.7 miles. Turn left into golf course entrance. Follow the park road about 0.5 mile to the parking lot next to the club house. Park at an empty area of the lot. Allow an hour and 15 minutes from Evanston.

title: Foley Pond (Neal Ney)
description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Moderate

Distance: 30 miles

Leader: Neal Ney

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/D5CB3ACE-D71C-4FB6-A5F9-5607FE8675EF_1_201_a.jpg!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/RWGPS-route.png!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/Foley%20Pond%20HP.jpg: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/44025534Tuesday's Moderate Ride: Foley Pond is a hidden gem of a park – essentially a fishing hole – in Highland Park. Most of us would never stumble across it in our usual comings and goings. It’s hard enough to find when you know that it is there. No parking lot, very subtle signage, no playground equipment, and no paved surfaces.

We’ll ride to Lake Forest along Ridge, take Old Elm and a few other streets east and south to Highland Park, stopping to lunch at Once Upon a Bagel. In part this route is an excuse to visit this old EBC favorite and Stanley-approved restaurant, that I don’t think we have visited enough lately. After lunch we’ll pay a short visit to Foley Pond. Fishing and birding are the major activities here, but we won’t be doing either. Just looking and maybe taking a couple of pictures.

title: The Perch Kitchen and Tap: Monthly Moderate Breakfast Club Ride (Sharon Kaminecki)
description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Moderate

Distance: Approx 20 miles

Leader: Sharon Kaminecki

## Monthly Moderate Breakfast Club Ride:

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/The%20Perch%20Kitchen%20and%20Tap.jpg: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/27045735If you like to eat, ride a bike, meet new people, explore different parts of the city but dedicate only half a day doing it at a time, this service of group bicycle rides is for you! The “Breakfast Club” will ride at a moderate pace to a different neighborhood stop for breakfast at a pre-selected restaurant, and be back around noon.

Join us as we ride to breakfast at **The Perch Kitchen and Tap**, 1932 W Division, Chicago in the Wicker Park neighborhood. The brunch menu includes omelets, fried egg sandwich, avocado toast, and lemon blueberry pancakes.

title: Family Day 2026
location: Gichigamiin Indigenous Nations Museum, 3001 Central Street, Evanston, IL, 60201, United States
description:
Join us for an afternoon of family crafts, storytelling, shop at our Artists Alley, enjoy live music, and so much more!

Throughout the event you can visit info booths to learn more about other incredible work being done by our partnering organizations. Visit our Artists Alley to shop at a handful of Native artists booths, enjoy live music, and visit our ongoing exhibits. This event will have something for everyone. Limited parking is available along Central Street and the parking lot behind the museum.

Full event details coming soon!

For more information about this program, please contact: info@gichigamiin.org | (847) 475-0911.

Riders must sign an up-to-date sign-in & waiver sheet (from EBC website) and there must be at least two people on the ride, with the leader being a current club member. Club members are usually available to lead these rides, but if no club member is available, the ride will not be held.**[](mailto:jigcsr@gmail.com)

**Very Fast Plus (22+ mph) Show & Go Ride** will be 40 to 100 miles, with frequent paceline work. The ride leaves promptly at 10 minutes after the start time. For questions about the ride: Rudy K 847-226-4574 rudy.kubica54@gmail.com

title: Middlefork Savanna (Neal Ney)
description:
Status: Confirmed

Pace: Moderate

Distance: 45 Miles

Leader: Neal Ney

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/RWGPS-route.png: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/27865884

!https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/MiddleforkSavanna.jpgThis week's Tuesday Moderate Ride: A savanna is open woodland with grasses and forbs growing under the trees. We will ride at a moderate pace to this rare remnant from Illinois' pre-settlement past. We'll ride a bit more than four miles on the limestone trail in the preserve at a slow pace with some stops for nature gawking. The trail is suitable for road bikes (as long as you are not in a hurry). After lunch at the Panera on 176 we will resume our moderate pace on the roads and paved paths back to Evanston.

title: Indigenous Peoples' Day
location: Gichigamiin Indigenous Nations Museum, 3001 Central Street, Evanston, IL, 60201, United States
description:
Indigenous Peoples’ Day honors the culture and history of Native people past, present, and future. Join us at Gichigamiin Indigenous Nations Museum to celebrate the diversity and vibrancy of Indigenous cultures with FREE admission to the museum all day. Bring your friends and your family to enrich your understanding of Indigenous histories and cultures and enjoy guided craft activities.
Full schedule of events coming soon!

For more information about this program, please contact: info@gichigamiin.org | (847) 475-0911

title: Dagwaagin (Fall) Artist Market - Small Business Saturday
location: Gichigamiin Indigenous Nations Museum, 3001 Central Street, Evanston, IL, 60201, United States
description:
Save the Date for our Dagwaagin (Fall) Artist Market – Small Business Saturday
This program honors Native American entrepreneurs and brings together innovative Native artists, business owners, leaders, and creators with community members, buyers, and potential clients. Participating Indigenous artists and entrepreneurs will have the chance to share their arts and crafts, contributions, accomplishments, and professional services with the public, allowing guests to support these Indigenous-owned companies by purchasing items or fostering future business opportunities.
Admission is FREE

Native Artists and Small Businesses
Start your holiday shopping early and show your support for local Native American Small Businesses this Small Business Saturday.

For more information about this program, please contact:
Josee Starr | jstarr@gichigamiin.org | (847) 475-0911

title: Lake Forest College Easter Brunch (Sara Polonsky)
description:
Status: PENDING!

Pace: Moderate

Distance: 32 miles

Leader: Sara Polonsky

# !https://ebcrides.org/images/cal/RWGPS-route.png: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/27000578

!http://myfrugaladventures.com/wp-content/uploads/2012/03/easter-basket.jpg**LEAVING PANERA @ 09:00 AM**. Join us at 9:00 am on Easter Sunday for a Moderate ride (13-15mph) to Lake Forest College for brunch. If the Easter Brunch Fast Ride is too fast for you, but you still want to enjoy the abundant brunch (for only $12.50) join us at Panera in Wilmette @ 9am for the Moderate version! Note: The ride will not happen if it is raining, snowing or otherwise miserable out.
Don't eat breakfast before you leave for this ride! This ride is back by popular demand after being on hiatus since 2019. For those of you who think inflation is now running rampant, **check this out**: Fabulous brunch (Lake Forest College students eat very, very well) - this year for a special deal of only **$12.50** (cash or credit card). (FYI: **2019**-$16.00; **2018**-$11.35; **2017**-$10.31; **2016**-9.92; **2015**-$9.55)

Distance: 40 miles

Leader: Timothy Schoolmaster
